**Commit**: `5ef398fcf426` — chore(redteam): close 7 MED + 4 LOW from prism-0.6.0 redteam pass

**Body**:

/redteam to convergence ran 4 rounds against the merged 0.6.0 release
(PRs #27-30). 0 CRIT, 0 HIGH at every round. Findings clustered in the
secondary-artefact layer (specs, CHANGELOG, analyses); code itself
shipped clean. Same-shard fix-immediately closed all actionable items
per autonomous-execution.md Rule 4.

- Type-system polish: __rowType phantom now type+runtime symmetric
  (use-filter-bar-state.ts), searchPlaceholder fallback handles
  whitespace (filter-bar.tsx), stale T[keyof T] cast removed
  (data-table-root.tsx) — contradicted the 0.6.0 unknown widening.
- Spec accuracy: phantom filter-bar.* tokens (14) and var(--prism-z-sticky)
  citation replaced with actual --prism-* tokens consumed by filter-bar.tsx,
  with literal fallback chains; vapourware claim reworded to JSDoc-only.
- Narrative coherence: CHANGELOG headline forward-tense ("expected to
  replace ~120 LOC … realised once arbor wave-6 ships"); Storybook
  overclaim reworded ("Storybook-compatible scenario exports …
  runner pending"); spec landing-site cited correctly (05m sub-spec,
  not parent 05).
- Label hygiene: bare BLOCKING-1/2 references qualified to
  M-XX BLOCKING-X across analyses + plan + CHANGELOG. Future grep on
  BLOCKING-1 now disambiguates by call site.

Convergence: 2 consecutive clean rounds (R3, R4) across security-reviewer,
reviewer, value-auditor agents. All 6 criteria met (0 CRIT/HIGH; spec
compliance 100% AST/grep verified; new code has 29 tests; 0 mock data).
